audit_items.proof_trace: All dynamic allocations in offload.c are freed on every execution path: `kmalloc` records in `nfp_map_ptr_record` are freed on error via `err_free_rec` and on teardown via `nfp_map_ptrs_forget`; partial `kzalloc` metas from `nfp_prog_prepare` are cleaned up by `nfp_prog_free` in the caller's error path; the `kvmalloc` buffer in `nfp_bpf_translate` (L233) is freed by `nfp_bpf_destroy` (L251) which is guaranteed to be called by the BPF framework on both success and translate-failure teardown paths; and the `img` allocation from `nfp_bpf_relo_for_vnic` is explicitly freed on both error (L530) and success (L545) paths in `nfp_net_bpf_load`.
